text_fr: A  la  meme  seance,  le  Conseil  a  en outre  decide, sur la demande des  representants  du  Burkina  Faso, de  l'Egypte et du  Zimbabwe 8 , d'adresser des invita› tions a M.  Mfanafuthi J. Makatini et a M.  Ahmed Gora Ebrahim en vertu de ]'article 39 du reglement interieur provisoire.
text_en: At the same time, the Council decided, at the request of the representatives of Burkina Faso, Egypt and Zimbabwe, to send invitations to Mr Mfanafouthi J. Makatini and to Mr Ahmed Gora Ebrahim under Article 39 of the provisional internal regulation.

text_fr: A  sa  2549e  seance,  le  16  aout  1984,  le  Conseil  a decide d'inviter les  representants du  Benin, de Cuba, de  la  Mongolie,  de  la  Republique  arabe  syrienne, de la Trinite-et-Tobago et de la Y ougoslavie a participer, sans droit de vote, a la discussion de  la question.
text_en: At its 2549th meeting, on 16 August 1984, the Council decided to invite the representatives of Benin, Cuba, Mongolia, the Syrian Arab Republic, Trinidad and Tobago and Yugoslavia to participate, without the right to vote, in the discussion of the question.

text_fr: A  la  meme  seance,  le  Conseil  a  egalement decide d'adresser une  invitation, en vertu de ]'article 39 du reglement interieur provisoire, au President du Comite special  charge  d'etudier la  situation  en  ce  qui  con› ceme l'application de  la  Declaration  sur l'octroi de l'independance aux pays et aux peuples coloniaux.
text_en: At the same meeting, the Council also decided to extend an invitation under Article 39 of the provisional rules of procedure to the Chairman of the Special Committee responsible for examining the situation in relation to the application of the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples.

text_fr: A  sa  2550e  seance,  le  17  aout  1984,  le  Conseil  a decide d'inviter les representants du Congo, de l'Indo› nesie,  du  Kowe'it, du  Qatar et de  Sri  Lanka a  parti› ciper, sans droit de vote, a la discussion de la question.
text_en: At its 2550th meeting, on 17 August 1984, the Council decided to invite the representatives of the Congo, Indo› nesie, Kuwait, Qatar and Sri Lanka to the discussion without the right to vote.

text_fr: A  sa  2551e  seance,  le  17  aout  1984,  le  Conseil  a decide  d'inviter les  representants  de  l' Afghanistan, du Guyana,  du  Kenya et du Togo a  participer,  sans droit de  vote, a la discussion de la question.
text_en: At its 2551st meeting, on 17 August 1984, the Council decided to invite the representatives of Afghanistan, Guyana, Kenya and Togo to participate, without the right to vote, in the discussion of the question.

text_fr: A la  meme  seance,  le  Conseil a  egalement decide, sur la  demande  des  representants  du  Burkina  Faso, de  l'Egypte et du  Zimbabwe 9 , d'adresser une  invita› tion a M. Lesaoana Makhanda en vertu de !'article 39 du reglement interieur provisoire.
text_en: At the same time, the Council also decided, at the request of the representatives of Burkina Faso, Egypt and Zimbabwe, to send an invitation to Mr Lesaoana Makhanda under Article 39 of the provisional internal regulation.

text_fr: Resolution 554 (1984)
text_en: Resolution 554 (1984)

text_fr: du  17 aout 1984
text_en: of 17 August 1984

text_fr: Le Conseil de  securite,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 473  (1980)  et la  resolution 38/11 de I' Assemblee generale, en date du 15 novembre 1983,  ainsi  que  les  autres  resolutions  pertinentes  de
text_en: Repe/ant its resolution 473 (1980) and resolution 38/11 of 15 November 1983 of the General Assembly, as well as other relevant resolutions of the

text_fr: !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  invitant  les  auto› rites sud-africaines a abandonner !'apartheid, a mettre fin  a  !'oppression et  a  la  repression  de  la  majorite noire  et  a  rechercheiune  solution  pacifique,  juste et durable  conformement aux  principes de  la Charte des Nations Unies et de la Declaration universelle des droits de l'homme,
text_en: !'United Nations inviting South African autoites to abandon!'apartheid, to put an end to the oppression and repression of the black majority and to seek a peaceful, just and lasting solution in accordance with the principles of the Charter of the United Nations and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights,

text_fr: Convaincu que  la  pretendue  "nouvelle  constitu› tion"  approuvee  le  2  novembre 1983 par l'electorat exclusivement blanc de I' Afrique du Sud maintiendrait le  processus  de  denationalisation de  la  majorite  afri› caine autochtone, la privant de  tous les droits fonda› mentaux,  et  renforcerait  encore !'apartheid, faisant de I' Afrique du Sud un  pays "reserve aux Blancs",
text_en: Convinced that the so-called "new constitution" approved on 2 November 1983 by the exclusively white electorate of South Africa would maintain the process of denationalisation of the indigenous African majority, depriving it of all fundamental rights, and would further strengthen apartheid, making South Africa a "white-only" country,

text_fr: Conscient que !'inclusion dans  la  "nouvelle cons› titution"  des  personnes  dites  "metis"  et  des  per› sonnes d'origine asiatique est destinee a briser !'unite du  peuple  opprime  d' Afrique  du  Sud  et  a  fomenter des conflits interieurs,
text_en: Aware that the inclusion in the "new constitution" of so-called "metis" people and people of Asian origin is intended to break down!'unite of the oppressed people of South Africa and to foment interior conflicts,

text_fr: Notant avec une  profonde inquietude que l'un des objectifs  de  la  pretendue  "constitution"  du  regime raciste est de  permettre  l'enrolement dans les  forces armees  du  regime d'apartheid des  "Metis"  et  des personnes d'origine asiatique  habitant en Afrique  du Sud  en  vue  d'accentuer la repression  a  l'interieur et d'augmenter Jes  actes  d'agression contre  des  Etats africains independants,
text_en: Noting with deep concern that one of the objectives of the "constitution" pretension of the racist regime is to allow the armed forces of the apartheid regime of the "Metis" and people of Asian origin living in South Africa to energize the internal repression and increase acts of aggression against independent African States,

text_fr: Se felicitant de  la  resistance  commune  massive  du peuple  opprime  d' Afrique  du  Sud  a  ces  manreuvres "constitutionnelles",
text_en: Welcoming the massive common resistance of the oppressed people of South Africa to these "constitutional" practices,

text_fr: Reaffirmant la  legitimite  de  la  Jutte  du  peuple opprime  d' Afrique  du  Sud  pour  !'elimination  de !'apartheid et pour l'instauration d'une societe  dans laquelle  tous  les  habitants de I' ensemble de I' Afrique du  Sud,  sans distinction de race, de couleur, de  sexe ou de croyance, jouiront en pleine egalite de tous les droits  politiques  et  autres  et  participeront  librement a  la determination de leur avenir,
text_en: Reaffirming the legitimacy of the Jutte of the oppressed people of South Africa for the elimination of apartheid and for the establishment of a society in which all the inhabitants of all South Africa, without distinction as to race, colour, sex or belief, shall enjoy in full equality of all political and other rights and shall participate freely in the determination of their future,

text_fr: Fermement  convaincu que  les  pretendues  "elec› tions"  qui  doivent  etre  organisees  par le  regime  de Pretoria  dans  le  courant  du  present  mois  pour  les "Metis" et les personnes d'origine asiatique et !'appli› cation  de  cette  "nouvelle  constitution"  aggraveront inevitablement Jes  tensions en Afrique du Sud et dans l'ensemble de  l'Afrique australe,
text_en: Strongly convinced that the pretensions "elec› tions" which must be organised by the Pretoria regime in the course of this month for the "Metis" and people of Asian origin and !'applying this "new constitution" will inevitably aggravate the tensions in South Africa and throughout southern Africa,

items_fr: l. Declare que  la  pretendue  "nouvelle  constitu› tion"  est  contraire  aux  principes  de  la  Charte  des Nations  Unies,  que  les  resultats  du  referendum  du 2  novembre 1983  soot denues de toute validite et que l' en tree  en  vigueur  de  la  "nouvelle constitution"  ne fera  qu'aggraver la  situation  deja explosive  existant en Afrique du Sud du fait  de !'apartheid;

2. Rejette energiquement et declare  nu/Jes  et non avenues la  pretendue  "nouvelle  constitution"  et  les "elections" qui doivent etre organisees dans le courant du  present  mois  pour  Jes  "Metis"  et  Jes  personnes d'origine asiatique,  ainsi  que  toutes  les  manreuvres insidieuses  du  regime  minoritaire  raciste  d' Afrique du  Sud  visant  a  renforcer  encore  le  pouvoir  blanc minoritaire et !'apartheid;
3. Rejette  egalement tout  pretendu  "reglement negocie'' fonde  sur la creation de  bantoustans ou  sur la pretendue "nouvelle constitution";

4. Declare  solennellement que  seules  l'eradica› tion  totale  de l'apartheid et l'instauration d'une so› ciete  democratique  sans distinction de race et fondee sur le principe du gouvemement par la majorite, grace au plein et libre exercice du droit de  vote par tous les adultes dans une Afrique du  Sud unie et non fra.gmen› tee, peuvent conduire a une solution juste et durable de la situation explosive qui regne en Afrique du  Sud;

5. Prie  instamment tous  les  gouvemements  et toutes  les  organisations  de  ne  pas  reconnaitre  les resultats  des  pretendues  "elections"  et, agissant en cooperation avec l'Organisation des Nations Unies et avec  l'Organisation de  }'unite  africaine,  de  prendre des  mesures  appropriees,  en  application  de  la  pre› sente resolution, afin d'aider le peuple opprime d' Afri› que  du  Sud  dans  sa lutte  legitime  pour l'instauration d'une societe democratique sans distinction de race;
6. Prie le  Secretaire  general  de  faire  rapport  au Conseil  de  securite  sur  l'application de  la  presente resolution;
?. Decide de rester saisi  de  la question.
items_en: l. Declares that the pretension "new constitution" is contrary to the principles of the Charter of the United Nations, that the results of the referendum of 2 November 1983 soot denues of all validity and that the entry into force of the "new constitution" will only aggravate the already explosive situation in South Africa as a result of apartheid;

2. Strongly rejects and declares nu/Is and not avenues the pretension "new constitution" and the "elections" which must be organized in the course of this month for Jes "Metis" and Jes personnes of Asian origin, as well as all the insidious maneuvers of the racist minority regime of South Africa aimed at further strengthening the minority white power and !'apartheid;
3. Rejects also any pretension "negotiated regulation" based on the creation of Bantustans or on the pretension "new constitution";

4. Declares solemnly that only the total eradication of apartheid and the establishment of a democratic society without distinction of race and based on the principle of majority rule, thanks to the full and free exercise of the right to vote by all adults in a united South Africa and not fra.gmen› tee, can lead to a just and lasting solution of the explosive situation in South Africa;

5. Urges all Governments and organizations not to recognize the results of the pretensions of elections and, in cooperation with the United Nations and the Organization of African Unity, to take appropriate measures, in accordance with the pre›fe resolution, to assist the oppressed people of Afri› as well as of the South in their legitimate struggle for the establishment of a democratic society without distinction as to race;
6. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Security Council on the implementation of the present resolution;
?. Decides to remain seized of the matter.

text_fr: Adoptee a la 2551'  seance par  /3  voix  contre  zero,  avec 2 abstentions (Etats-Unis d'Amirique et Royaume› Uni  de  Grande-Bretagne  et d'Jr/ande du  Nord).
text_en: Adopted by 3 votes to 0, with 2 abstentions (United States of America and United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland).

text_fr: Decisions
text_en: Decisions

text_fr: Asa 2560e  seance,  le  23  octobre  1984,  le  Conseil a decide d'inviter les  representants de 1' Afrique du  Sud et de  l'Ethiopie a participer,  sans  droit  de  vote, a la discussion  de  la  question  intitulee  "La question  de I' Afrique  du  Sud  : lettre, en date du  17  octobre  1984, adressee  au  President  du  Conseil  de  securite  par  le representant  permanent  de l'Ethiopie  aupres  de l'Organisation des  Nations Unies (S/16786 10 )''.
text_en: Asa 2560th meeting, on 23 October 1984, the Council decided to invite the representatives of South Africa and Ethiopia to participate, without the right to vote, in the discussion of the question entitled "The question of South Africa: letter dated 17 October 1984 addressed to the President of the Security Council by the Permanent Representative of Ethiopia to the United Nations (S/16786 10).'

text_fr: A la  meme  seance,  le  Conseil  a egalement decide d'adresser une  invitation,  en vertu de  l'article 39  du reglement interieur provisoire, au President du Comite special contre I' apartheid.
text_en: At the same time, the Council also decided to extend an invitation under Article 39 of the provisional rules of procedure to the Chairman of the Special Committee against Apartheid.

text_fr: A la meme seance, le  Conseil a decide en outre, sur la  demande  des  representants  du  Burkina  Faso,  de
text_en: At the same time, the Council also decided, at the request of the representatives of Burkina Faso, to

text_fr: I'Egypte et du Zimbabwe 11 , d'adresser une  invitation a l'eveque Desmond Tutu en  vertu de  l'article 39  du reglement interieur provisoire.
text_en: Egypt and Zimbabwe 11 , to extend an invitation to the Bishop Desmond Tutu under Article 39 of the Provisional Internal Regulation.

text_fr: Resolution 556 (1984)
text_en: Resolution 556 (1984)

text_fr: du 23 octobre 1984
text_en: of 23 October 1984

text_fr: Le Conseil de  securite,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 554 (1984) et les resolutions 38/11  et 39/2 de 1' Assemblee generale, en date respec› tivement des  15  novembre 1983  et 28  septembre 1984, qui  ont declare  la pretendue  "nouvelle constitution" contraire  aux  principes  de  la  Charle  des  Nations Unies,
text_en: Repe/ant its resolution 554 (1984) and resolutions 38/11 and 39/2 of the General Assembly, of 15 November 1983 and 28 September 1984, which declared the pretension "new constitution" contrary to the principles of the United Nations Charle,

text_fr: Reaffirmant les  dispositions  de  la  Declaration  uni› verselle  des  droits  de  l'homme, et plus  particuliere› ment  les  paragraphes  1 et  3  de  l' article  21,  qui  dis› posent  notamment que  toute  personne  a  le  droit  de prendre  part a la direction  des  affaires  publiques  de son pays, soit directement, soit par l'intermediaire de representants librement choisis,  et que  la  volonte  du peuple  est  le  fondement  de  l'autorite des  pouvoirs publics,
text_en: Reaffirming the provisions of the United Declaration of Human Rights, and in particular paragraphs 1 and 3 of Article 21, which state, inter alia, that everyone has the right to take part in the conduct of public affairs in his country, either directly or through the intermediary of freely chosen representatives, and that the will of the people is the basis of the authority of the public authorities,

text_fr: Alarme par l'aggravation de  la situation en  Afrique du Sud, en particulier par le  meurtre gratuit et la muti› lation  de  manifestants  sans  defense  et de  travailleurs en  greve,  ainsi  que  par  l'instauration de  fait  d'une situation  de  loi  martiale  visant a faciliter  la  repres› sion brutale de  la population noire,
text_en: Alarmed by the worsening of the situation in South Africa, in particular by the gratuitous murder and mutilation of defenceless demonstrators and workers in strike, as well as by the de facto establishment of a martial law situation aimed at facilitating the brutal representation of the black population,

text_fr: Gravement  preoccupe par  la  poursuite  des  arres› tations  et  des  detentions  arbitraires,  sans  jugement, de dirigeants  et  d'animateurs  d'organisations  de masse  dans  le  pays,  ainsi  que  par  la  fermeture  de plusieurs ecoles et universites,
text_en: Gravely concerned with the continuation of arbitrary arrests and detentions, without trial, of leaders and leaders of mass organizations in the country, as well as the closure of several schools and universities,

text_fr: Felicitant le  peuple opprime d' Afrique du  Sud de sa resistance  unie  et massive a la  pretendue  "nouvelle constitution"  qui  Jui  est  imposee,  notamment  de  la greve de  centaines de  milliers  d'etudiants et d'eleves noirs,
text_en: Commending the oppressed people of South Africa for their united and massive resistance to the "new constitution" that Jui is imposing, including the strike of hundreds of thousands of black students and students,

text_fr: Felicitant  aussi Jes communautes  asiatique  et metisse  d' Afrique  du  Sud  de  leur  boycottage  massif des  recentes  "elections", qui  constitue  une  repudia› tion manifeste de la pretendue "nouvelle constitution".
text_en: Also congratulating the Asian community and putting South Africa on their massive boycott of the recent elections, which constitutes a clear repudiation of the pretension of the "new constitution".

text_fr: Reaffirmant la legitimite  de  la  Jutte  du  peuple opprime  d' Afrique  du  Sud  pour le  plein  exercice  de son droit a l'autodetermination et l'instauration d'une societe  democratique  sans  distinction  de  race  dans une  Afrique du Sud non fragmentee,
text_en: Reaffirming the legitimacy of the Jutte of the oppressed people of South Africa for the full exercise of their right to self-determination and the establishment of a democratic society without distinction as to race in a non-fragmented South Africa,

text_fr: Convaincu que  I' Afrique  du  Sud,  en  faisant  fi  de l'opinion publique  mondiale  et  en  imposant  la  pre› tendue "nouvelle constitution", qui a ete rejetee, pro› voquera  inevitablement  une  nouvelle  aggravation
text_en: Convinced that South Africa, by ignoring world public opinion and imposing the pre-stressed "new constitution", which has been rejected, will inevitably bring about a further worsening
